mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer spending and construction activity, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, triggering short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the regime-conditional effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 8%, aggressive central bank rate hikes become inevitable, forcing rapid equity repricing and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are non-negative in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the threshold effect shows no statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains wage-driven consumption and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01 over any 3-year window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll additions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates a shift from durable goods to services, creating sector rotation volatility that suppresses broad index returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to outperform single-proxy models in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase confirms supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of recovery-phase observations with rising INDPRO, or the regime filter adds no predictive alpha.
